steps.title: Look for a decline over time
steps.content: The claim is that some lakes saw a decline in the duration of surface ice between the early 1980s and the mid-2000s. The supporting data must compare the same lake across those two periods and show fewer ice days in the later winter.

steps.title: Avoid unrelated comparisons
steps.content: Choice A compares two different lakes and latitude, not a decline over time. Choice B shows Lake Baikal had more ice days later, which is an increase. Choice D also compares two lakes and typical duration rather than the same lake across years. The answer is C.
